data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me collect the packages:

From Core.csproj:
- System.Text.Json 9.0.0

From Tests.csproj:
- Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.12.0
- xunit 2.9.3
- xunit.runner.visualstudio 2.8.2 (PrivateAssets="all")

From Web.csproj:
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting 1.15.0
- Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi 8.0.24

No version conflicts here - each package appears only once across all projects. I need to:


1. Create a Directory.Packages.props file in the solution root that enables centralized version management and declares all the package versions.
2. Strip the version attributes from the PackageReference elements in each .csproj file, keeping the PrivateAssets attribute on xunit.runner.visualstudio.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi" Version="8.0.24" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="9.0.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
Done. Here's what I did:

**Created `Directory.Packages.props`** at the solution root with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` enabled and all 6 package versions centralized:

| Package | Version |
|---|---|
| Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi | 8.0.24 |
| Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk | 17.12.0 |
| OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | 1.15.0 |
| System.Text.Json | 9.0.0 |
| xunit | 2.9.3 |
| xunit.runner.visualstudio | 2.8.2 |

**Updated 3 `.csproj` files** — removed `Version` attributes from all `PackageReference` entries while preserving other attributes like `PrivateAssets="all"`.

No version conflicts existed — each package appeared in only one project.
